session_hint: the Stripe webhook duplicate charge investigation
question: What was causing duplicate charges from Stripe webhook events?
turns: We're seeing duplicate charges for ~2% of transactions. Each charge appears exactly twice in our DB, 35 seconds apart. First theory: our HTTP client has automatic retry configured and is resending the payment confirmation request.

Audited the Axios config in payment-service — no retry adapter installed, timeout set to 10 seconds, no interceptors that retry on failure. Checked nginx access logs: only one inbound request per duplicate pair. HTTP client retry is not the cause. Ruling it out.

Second theory: the AWS ALB health check is hitting /webhooks/stripe accidentally alongside /health. Checked ALB target group config — health check path is strictly /health and the access log shows no health check requests hitting the webhook endpoint. Ruling out health check interference.

Dug into the Stripe dashboard event logs. Stripe sends a webhook and waits for a 200 response within 30 seconds. Our handler does synchronous payment processing that takes 32-40 seconds before returning 200. Stripe interprets the delay as a delivery failure and retries the event. We never check the Stripe event ID for idempotency before processing. Added a guard: before processing, query the charges table for the Stripe event_id — if already processed, return 200 immediately. Duplicates dropped to zero. Root cause: missing idempotency check on Stripe event_id combined with slow handler triggering Stripe's own retry.
